data.title: Trump Jr Says AI Agents That Can Think But Can't Pay Are Just 'Expensive Interns' — Touts New World Liberty Financial Infrastructure
data.description: Donald Trump Jr. recently endorsed World Liberty Financial's (WLFI) new payment infrastructure that allows AI agents to handle funds and conduct transactions on the blockchain. Trump Jr. highlighted the AgentPay Software Development Kit, describing it as "open source, self-custodial, and policy-first," suggesting that it could facilitate settlements for transactions as low as USD1. He emphasized the importance of AI agents being able to make payments efficiently, stating that "AI agents that can reason but can't pay for anything are just expensive interns." World Liberty Financial has officially launched this infrastructure, enabling agents to transfer and trade funds programmatically based on pre-defined rules enforced by WLFI. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Alstom S.A : Alstom signs a 5-year contract extension to operate and maintain the GO Transit and UP express fleets in Toronto, Canada
data.description: Alstom has recently secured a significant contract extension in Toronto, Canada, agreeing to a 5-year deal to oversee the operation and maintenance of the GO Transit and UP Express fleets. This new agreement underscores the company's commitment to providing transportation services in the region.

data.title: Intellicheck (IDN) Stock Jumps 15% After Hours: Here's What You Should Know
data.description: Intellicheck Inc. (NASDAQ: IDN) saw its shares surge by 15% to $5.52 in after-hours trading following the announcement of its record-breaking fourth-quarter revenue and inaugural annual net profit for the fiscal year that ended on December 31, 2025. The company's Q4 revenue reached an all-time high, climbing 12% compared to the previous year to $6.63 million, up from $5.93 million in Q4 of 2024. The Software as a Service (SaaS) revenue, which signifies the recurring revenue from cloud-based applications via monthly or annual subscriptions, made up nearly the entire fourth-quarter revenue and increased by 12% to $6.62 million. Intellicheck disclosed that its gross margin stood firm at 91.4%, while operating expenditures fell by 7% to $4.57 million. For the first time ever, Intellicheck reported an annual profit for the year 2025, with a net income of $1.27 million, translating to $0.06 per diluted share.

data.title: "Retail investors lose on personal portfolios despite index gains due to market structure, sector rotation, and biases."
data.description: During bull markets, retail investors often experience the dilemma of outperforming the index but still losing in their own portfolios. This phenomenon is explored in this essay across four main aspects: the phased nature of bull markets, the pattern of sector rotation, psychological biases influencing investment behavior, and technical indicators for determining selling points. The key point emphasized is that losses in a bull market are usually not due to incorrect market predictions, but rather stem from a lack of understanding of the market's internal dynamics and succumbing to personal psychological tendencies. These factors are crucial for investors to consider in order to navigate bull markets successfully.

data.description: On March 12, 2026, the 2026 London Book Fair concluded at Olympia London, serving as a significant global platform for Chinese publishers to present their recent accomplishments. This event, considered one of the premier publishing gatherings worldwide, attracted a large audience and facilitated the exchange of ideas among industry professionals.
